title: openwashdata package standards
text: Standard version: 1.0.0 Source: https://github.com/openwashdata/pkgreview package reviewed openwashdata standards recorded file. guides every future Claude session working package, including sessions student contributors external collaborators review tooling installed. delete file: record standard version package reviewed . /review-package command writes review start; version pins standard whole review. Note: pkgdown renders every top-level .md file, file appears package website CLAUDE.html. accepted openwashdata behavior (publicly records standard package reviewed ); pkgdown offers configuration exclude . delete file hide .

what: Rules a future session must not undo
title: openwashdata package standards
text: Vignettes live vignettes/articles/, never directly vignettes/. keeps pkgdown rendering correct avoids CRAN issues. _pkgdown.yml follows standard openwashdata configuration , including Plausible analytics header ETH funding sidebar. remove reorganize blocks. Analysis, validation, testing scripts live analysis/ package root. intentionally outside R/ built installed package. move delete ; exist reproducibility. license CC 4.0. change . Missing values coded NA, never empty strings, “NULL”, “N/”, sentinel numbers -99. editing DESCRIPTION, run washr::update_description(). Caveat (washr 1.0.1): strips Config/Needs/website entries; diff DESCRIPTION call restore anything removed. version author changes, run washr::update_citation() DESCRIPTION, CITATION.cff, inst/CITATION stay sync. Caveats (washr 1.0.1): doi argument required, call package DOI doi = NULL DOI exists; doi = NULL can inject broken empty badge (zenodo.org/badge/DOI/.svg) README.Rmd, must removed; leaves inst/CITATION.bk1 backup files must committed. Raw data stays data-raw/, processed .rda data data/, CSV/XLSX exports inst/extdata/. data-raw/dictionary.csv documents variables.

title: openwashdata package standards
text: Multi-dataset packages: one .rda data/ one roxygen .R file per dataset, unique, descriptive name; dataset named package. _pkgdown.yml reference index lists every dataset.
code: package-name/ ├── DESCRIPTION ├── NAMESPACE ├── R/package-name.R          # roxygen data documentation ├── data/package-name.rda     # single dataset: named after the package ├── data-raw/ │   ├── data_processing.R │   └── dictionary.csv ├── inst/ │   ├── CITATION │   └── extdata/              # CSV and XLSX exports ├── man/ ├── vignettes/articles/       # all vignettes go here ├── analysis/                 # analysis scripts, not built ├── README.Rmd / README.md ├── NEWS.md ├── CITATION.cff ├── _pkgdown.yml └── .github/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml

title: openwashdata package standards
text: Replace packagename actual package name. url site base URL (canonical links, sitemap.xml, redirects), must Pages URL, never GitHub repo URL; repo link lives home.links. Multi-dataset packages: list one entry per data object contents, unique, descriptive name; none named package.
code: url: https://openwashdata.github.io/packagename/ template:   bootstrap: 5   includes:     in_header: |       <script defer data-domain="openwashdata.github.io" src="https://plausible.io/js/script.js"></script>  home:   links:     - icon: github       text: GitHub repository       href: https://github.com/openwashdata/packagename   sidebar:     structure: [links, citation, authors, dev, custom]     components:       custom:         title: Funding         text: This project was funded by the [Open Research Data Program of the ETH Board](https://ethrat.ch/en/eth-domain/open-research-data/).  authors:   footer:     roles: [cre, fnd]     text: "Crafted by"   sidebar:     roles: [cre, aut, ctb]     before: "So *who* does the work?"     after: "Thanks all!"  reference: - title: "Data"   desc: "Access the packagename dataset"   contents:   - packagename

title: openwashdata package standards
text: 2 spaces indentation, tabs; maximum 80 characters per line tidyverse style R code; snake_case functions variables

title: openwashdata package standards
text: Rebuild README: R -e "devtools::build_readme()" Rebuild documentation: R -e "devtools::document()" Full check: R -e "devtools::check()" Build website: R -e "pkgdown::build_site()"

title: Authors and Citation
text: Alex Y. Katukiza. Author. Charles B. Niwagaba. Author. Ivan Feni. Author. Shaluwa Namagembe. Author. Swaib Semiyaga. Author. Abubakar Batte. Author. Musa Manga. Author. Lars Schöbitz. Author, maintainer. Global Health Engineering, ETH Zurich. Funder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Katukiza , Niwagaba C, Feni , Namagembe S, Semiyaga S, Batte , Manga M, Schöbitz L (2026). “solidwastekampala: Quantity Composition Domestic Solid Waste Kampala City.” https://openwashdata.github.io/solidwastekampala/.
code: @Misc{katukiza_etall:2026,   title = {solidwastekampala: Quantity and Composition of Domestic Solid Waste in Kampala City},   author = {Alex Y. Katukiza and Charles B. Niwagaba and Ivan Feni and Shaluwa Namagembe and Swaib Semiyaga and Abubakar Batte and Musa Manga and Lars Schöbitz},   year = {2026},   url = {https://openwashdata.github.io/solidwastekampala/},   abstract = {Domestic solid waste generation and composition data for 103 households in Kampala City, Uganda, collected over a seven-day measurement campaign in three parishes representing different income levels (Bwaise I, low income; Bukoto I, middle income; Ggaba, high income). Includes ten measured waste-category masses, derived per-household metrics, and household socio-economic characteristics. The data accompany the manuscript "Quantity and Composition of Domestic Solid Waste in Kampala City as Influenced by Socio-Economic Factors" (Katukiza et al., Makerere University).},   version = {0.0.0.9000}, }

what: Quantity and Composition of Domestic Solid Waste in Kampala City
title: Quantity and Composition of Domestic Solid Waste in Kampala City
text: solidwastekampala package provides household-level data behind manuscript “Quantity Composition Domestic Solid Waste Kampala City Influenced Socio-Economic Factors” (Katukiza et al., Makerere University). seven-day measurement campaign, study team weighed domestic solid waste generated 103 households three Kampala parishes selected represent different income levels: Bwaise (low), Bukoto (middle), Ggaba (high). record combines measured mass ten waste categories derived per-household metrics socio-economic characteristics household dwelling. package contains one analysis-ready dataset together full variable dictionary.

title: Quantity and Composition of Domestic Solid Waste in Kampala City
text: can install development version solidwastekampala GitHub : Alternatively, can download individual datasets CSV XLSX file table . Click Download CSV. window opens displays CSV browser. Right-click anywhere inside window select “Save Page …”. Save file folder choice.
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("openwashdata/solidwastekampala") ## Run the following code in console if you don't have the packages ## install.packages(c("dplyr", "knitr", "readr", "stringr", "gt", "kableExtra")) library(dplyr) library(knitr) library(readr) library(stringr) library(gt) library(kableExtra)

title: Quantity and Composition of Domestic Solid Waste in Kampala City
text: package provides access one dataset waste measurements socio-economic characteristics 103 analyzed households.

title: Quantity and Composition of Domestic Solid Waste in Kampala City
text: dataset solidwastekampala contains mass ten waste categories collected household seven-day period, derived per-household metrics (total, per-day, per-capita, volume, density), socio-economic characteristics household dwelling. 103 observations 37 variables. overview variable names, see following table.

title: Quantity and Composition of Domestic Solid Waste in Kampala City
text: example reproduces headline figures manuscript: mean per-capita waste generation mean waste density income level. figure boxplot-per-capita shows distribution behind means: per-capita waste generation rises income level study area, 0.43 kg per person per day Bwaise (low) 0.98 kg Ggaba (high), spread across households also widest.
code: library(solidwastekampala) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2)  solidwastekampala |>   group_by(income_level) |>   summarise(     households = n(),     waste_kg_capita_day = round(mean(waste_per_capita_kg), 2),     density_kg_m3 = round(mean(density_kg_l) * 1000)   ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   income_level households waste_kg_capita_day density_kg_m3 #>   <ord>             <int>               <dbl>         <dbl> #> 1 low                  39                0.43           368 #> 2 middle               32                0.53           571 #> 3 high                 32                0.98           325 ggplot(solidwastekampala,        aes(x = income_level, y = waste_per_capita_kg)) +   geom_boxplot() +   labs(     x = "Income level of study area",     y = "Waste generation (kg per person per day)",     title = "Per-capita waste generation by income level"   ) +   theme_minimal()

what: solidwastekampala: Quantity and Composition of Domestic Solid Waste in Kampala City — solidwastekampala
title: solidwastekampala: Quantity and Composition of Domestic Solid Waste in Kampala City — solidwastekampala
text: Household-level results seven-day domestic solid waste characterisation three parishes Kampala City, Uganda (Bwaise = low income, Bukoto = middle income, Ggaba = high income). 103 analyzed households data record socio-economic characteristics, measured mass ten waste categories, derived per-household metrics (total, per-day, per-capita, volume, density).

title: solidwastekampala: Quantity and Composition of Domestic Solid Waste in Kampala City — solidwastekampala
solidwastekampala: Quantity and Composition of Domestic Solid Waste in Kampala City — solidwastekampala
text: tibble 103 rows 37 variables id Package-level unique household identifier (1-103), following row numbering All_data sheet raw workbook (Bwaise , Bukoto , Ggaba). household_id Household label recorded area sheet (example Household_7); values repeat across parishes contain gaps, id unique key. division Kampala City division household located (Kawempe, Makindye, Nakawa). parish Study parish household located (Bwaise = low income, Bukoto = middle income, Ggaba = high income area). zone Administrative zone within parish household located. income_level Income level household's study area classified study design; ordered factor levels low < middle < high. occupants Number people living household 7-day waste collection period. age_head Age household head years; ordered factor levels 18-35 < 36-60 < >60. gender_head Gender household head (Female Male). education_head Highest education level household head; ordered factor levels Illiterate < Primary < Secondary < Tertiary. profession_head Profession household head (Business, Government employee, Housewife, Private employee, Retired). monthly_income_ugx Monthly household income band Uganda shillings (UGX); ordered factor levels <500,000 < 500,000-1,000,000 < 1,000,000-3,000,000 < >3,000,000. respondent Household member answered questionnaire (Household head, Spouse, Son/daughter, Niece/nephew). period_of_stay long household lived premises; ordered factor levels <1 year < 1-3 years < 3-5 years < >5 years. occupancy Occupancy status premises (Occupied owner Rented). housing_quality Type dwelling proxy housing quality (Bungalow, Story building, Temporary). roof_material Main roof material dwelling (Cemented, Iron sheets, Iron sheets/tiles, Tiles); Bwaise recorded combined Iron sheets/tiles category. wall_material Main wall material dwelling (Bricks, Concrete blocks, Mud/poles). floor_material Main floor material dwelling (Cemented, Mud, Tiles). water_access Main drinking water access household (House connection, Stand pipe, Yard tap). sanitation_facility Whether household's sanitation facility shared households (Shared facilities shared facilities). road_condition Condition access road household (Paved Unpaved). waste_food_kg Mass food waste generated household 7-day collection period, kilograms (ASTM waste category). waste_garden_kg Mass garden waste generated household 7-day collection period, kilograms (ASTM waste category). waste_wood_kg Mass wood waste generated household 7-day collection period, kilograms (ASTM waste category). waste_textiles_kg Mass textile waste generated household 7-day collection period, kilograms (ASTM waste category). waste_paper_kg Mass paper waste generated household 7-day collection period, kilograms (ASTM waste category). waste_polythene_kg Mass polythene waste generated household 7-day collection period, kilograms (ASTM waste category). waste_plastics_kg Mass plastic waste generated household 7-day collection period, kilograms (ASTM waste category). waste_glass_kg Mass glass waste generated household 7-day collection period, kilograms (ASTM waste category). waste_metals_kg Mass metal waste generated household 7-day collection period, kilograms (ASTM waste category); Ggaba Household_7 value 2.674 stored text raw sheet excluded household's recorded waste_total_kg. waste_other_kg Mass waste covered nine categories, generated household 7-day collection period, kilograms (ASTM waste category). waste_total_kg Total mass waste generated household 7-day collection period, kilograms, recorded raw sheet (SUM ten category columns). waste_per_day_kg Mean daily mass waste generated household, kilograms per day (waste_total_kg divided 7). waste_per_capita_kg Mean daily mass waste generated per household member, kilograms per person per day (waste_per_day_kg divided occupants). volume_l Volume waste generated household 7-day collection period, litres. density_kg_l Bulk density generated waste, kilograms per litre; multiply 1000 kg/m3 reported manuscript.

title: solidwastekampala: Quantity and Composition of Domestic Solid Waste in Kampala City — solidwastekampala
text: Data collected study team Katukiza et al. (Makerere University) manuscript "Quantity Composition Domestic Solid Waste Kampala City Influenced Socio-Economic Factors". Raw workbook preserved data-raw/data_sheets_with_analysis.xlsx.

title: solidwastekampala: Quantity and Composition of Domestic Solid Waste in Kampala City — solidwastekampala
code: head(solidwastekampala) #> # A tibble: 6 × 37 #>      id household_id division parish   zone     income_level occupants age_head #>   <int> <chr>        <chr>    <chr>    <chr>    <ord>            <int> <ord>    #> 1     1 Household_1  Kawempe  Bwaise I Lubagge  low                  3 18-35    #> 2     2 Household_2  Kawempe  Bwaise I Lubagge  low                  7 36-60    #> 3     3 Household_3  Kawempe  Bwaise I Lubagge  low                  6 36-60    #> 4     4 Household_5  Kawempe  Bwaise I Bubajjwe low                 11 >60      #> 5     5 Household_6  Kawempe  Bwaise I Bubajjwe low                  2 18-35    #> 6     6 Household_7  Kawempe  Bwaise I Bubajjwe low                  7 18-35    #> # ℹ 29 more variables: gender_head <chr>, education_head <ord>, #> #   profession_head <chr>, monthly_income_ugx <ord>, respondent <chr>, #> #   period_of_stay <ord>, occupancy <chr>, housing_quality <chr>, #> #   roof_material <chr>, wall_material <chr>, floor_material <chr>, #> #   water_access <chr>, sanitation_facility <chr>, road_condition <chr>, #> #   waste_food_kg <dbl>, waste_garden_kg <dbl>, waste_wood_kg <dbl>, #> #   waste_textiles_kg <dbl>, waste_paper_kg <dbl>, waste_polythene_kg <dbl>, … summary(solidwastekampala$waste_per_capita_kg) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #> 0.01629 0.30652 0.57279 0.62972 0.82521 1.94374  table(solidwastekampala$income_level) #>  #>    low middle   high  #>     39     32     32
